name: GR 123: Tour of Biscay
name_original: Faro de la Galea dede Sopela
description: Jog this moderate 4.9-mile (7.8 km) coastal route from Sopela to Faro de la Galea, enjoying panoramic ocean views and historic landmarks.
region_intro: Jogging around Sopelana offers a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by its coastal setting. The region features prominent cliffs, expansive beaches, and a network of trails that provide views of the Bay of Biscay. Runners can explore routes that traverse both the shoreline and inland areas, encountering varied terrain. The elevation changes are generally moderate, making the area accessible for different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Sopelana The most popular jogging route is GR 123: Tour of Biscay, a 4.9 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a scenic journey through the region. Another top favourite among local runners is Punta Galea Cliffs – Azkorri Beach View loop from Sopela, a difficult 10.8 miles (17.4 km) path. This route provides expansive views along the cliffs and coastline, including Azkorri Beach. Local runners also love the View of Sopela Coast – Muriola Beach loop from Larrabasterra, a 9.0 miles (14.4 km) trail leading through coastal paths and past Muriola Beach, often completed in about 1 hour 47 minutes. Jogging around Sopelana is defined by its coastal paths, dramatic cliffs, and sandy beaches. The network of running routes offers options for easy, moderate, and more advanced ability levels. The routes in Sopelana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1000 runners have used komoot to explore Sopelana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Sopelana?
A: There are over 40 dedicated running routes around Sopelana, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels. These include 2 easy routes, 32 moderate routes, and 8 difficult routes, ensuring options for all runners.

Q: Are there any easy jogging paths suitable for beginners in Sopelana?
A: Yes, Sopelana offers a few easy jogging paths perfect for beginners. While specific named easy routes aren't highlighted in the top selections, the region generally features moderate elevation changes, making many sections accessible. Look for routes with shorter distances and minimal elevation gain for a gentle start.

Q: What are some challenging long-distance running trails in Sopelana?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Sopelana has several difficult long-distance trails. One such route is the &lt;strong&gt;Muriola Beach – Plentzia Pedestrian Bridge loop from Larrabasterra&lt;/strong&gt;, which spans approximately 18 km (11.2 miles) and features significant elevation changes. You can explore this route here: &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3991467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muriola Beach – Plentzia Pedestrian Bridge loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Sopelana?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Sopelana are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;strong&gt;View of Sopela Coast – Muriola Beach loop from Sopela&lt;/strong&gt;, a moderate 10.1 km (6.3 miles) route offering coastal views. Find more details about this loop here: &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10512372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Sopela Coast – Muriola Beach loop from Sopela&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I expect to see while running in Sopelana?
A: Sopelana's coastal location means many running routes offer breathtaking views of the Bay of Biscay, dramatic cliffs, and expansive beaches. Trails often traverse elevated sections providing panoramic vistas of the coastline and the surrounding natural landscape.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Sopelana?
A: The running trails in Sopelana are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Runners frequently praise the diverse coastal paths, the dramatic cliff scenery, and the variety of routes suitable for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any historical or natural landmarks to see along the running routes in Sopelana?
A: Yes, you can encounter several interesting landmarks. For instance, the region is home to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1516121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Butrón Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a medieval fortress, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/566248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Askorriaga Fortress&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass by natural highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/569716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barinatxe - The Wild Beach&lt;/a&gt; or even catch glimpses of summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4198198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saierri Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Sopelana suitable for family-friendly jogging?
A: Sopelana offers routes with moderate elevation changes, making some sections suitable for families. While there aren't specific 'family-friendly' designated jogging routes, shorter, flatter sections along the beaches or less strenuous coastal paths can be enjoyed by families. Always check the route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the jogging trails in Sopelana?
A: Many outdoor areas in Sopelana, including some trails, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in nature reserves or populated areas, and to check local regulations for specific beaches or protected zones you might pass through. Always clean up after your pet.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Sopelana?
A: Sopelana enjoys a mild oceanic climate, making it suitable for running for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds. Summer can be warm, especially midday, but coastal breezes often provide relief. Winter running is also possible, though you should be prepared for cooler temperatures and potential rain.

Q: Are there any jogging routes that pass by the Sopelana bunker?
A: While the guide doesn't specifically detail routes passing directly by the Sopelana bunker, many trails traverse the coastal areas and cliffs where such historical structures are located. Exploring routes along the coastline, particularly those with views of the Bay of Biscay, may bring you close to these points of interest.

Q: How accessible are Sopelana's running routes by public transport?
A: Sopelana is well-connected by public transport, particularly the metro (Line 1) which has a station in Sopelana itself. This makes many starting points for running routes easily accessible, allowing runners to reach the trails without a car. Check local transport schedules for specific route access.
tips: Very clean well maintained bunker with graffiti.
Views of the cliffs around the Punta Galea lighthouse
The lighthouse is located at the entrance to the Bilbao estuary. It is a beautiful walk along the cliffs and the Cantabrian sea. There is an exercise area and benches to rest looking at the sea.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Faro de la Galea from Sopela route?
faqs.answer: The route features a varied terrain, combining both paved and natural paths. While generally accessible, expect some unpaved and gravel sections. There are also some hilly parts, particularly closer to Sopela, including a notably steep decline and a staircase between Gorrondatxe Hondartza and Sopela Beach. A section near the lighthouse is quite straight and includes a parallel path.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks and viewpoints can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: This scenic route offers continuous panoramic views of the Atlantic Ocean and the Cantabrian Sea. Key landmarks include the Punta Galea Lighthouse, the majestic Punta Galea Cliffs, and the 18th-century Fuerte de la Galea. You'll also pass the Aixerrota Windmill, Barinatxe ("La Salvaje") Beach, and Gorrondatxe/Azkorri Beach. On clear days, you might even see the coastline of Cantabria.

faqs.question: How can I get to the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The route is easily accessible via public transport. The Bidezábal metro station is a convenient option. If you're driving, there is parking available near Sopela and Punta Galea, though specific parking areas may vary.

faqs.question: Is the Faro de la Galea from Sopela route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is generally considered of moderate difficulty for jogging, requiring a good level of fitness. While many sections are accessible, some parts necessitate sure-footedness due to varied terrain and some steep inclines/declines. Beginners with some prior jogging experience might find it manageable, but those new to jogging may find it challenging.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this coastal route?
faqs.answer: Spring and early summer are ideal for jogging this route, as you can enjoy pleasant weather and see bright yellow wildflowers along the path. The route is also popular for its celebrated sunsets, making late afternoon jogs particularly rewarding.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome on this coastal route. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially near cliffs, other trail users, and protected natural areas to ensure safety and respect for wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee to access this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay an entrance fee to access the Faro de la Galea from Sopela route. It is a publicly accessible coastal path.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of other official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Bizkaiko bira, Azkorri bidea, Gorrondatxe-Azkorri bidea, and Galea errepidea during your journey.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like benches or exercise areas along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is well-equipped for outdoor activity. You will find convenient exercise areas and benches strategically placed along the path, offering opportunities for rest and enjoying the views.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while jogging?
faqs.answer: The route is renowned for its breathtaking scenery, offering continuous panoramic views of the Atlantic Ocean and the Cantabrian Sea. You'll pass impressive rocky cliffs, various beaches like Barinatxe and Gorrondatxe/Azkorri, and enjoy vistas of the Bilbao estuary and El Abra Port. Keep an eye out for bright yellow wildflowers and activities like model airplane flights and paragliding.

faqs.question: Is there a specific section that offers particularly good views for photography?
faqs.answer: The entire route offers stunning photographic opportunities, especially around the View of the Punta Galea Cliffs and near the Punta Galea Lighthouse. Sunsets from this route are particularly celebrated and provide spectacular backdrops.

waypoints.name: View of the Punta Galea Cliffs
